leadOwned.reason: Mutable workflow state, packet publication and operation artifacts belong to the controller.
Controller-owned refreeze support for preserving compatible accepted tasks across plan adoption.
Controller-owned CLI flag for compatible accepted-task preservation during refreeze adoption.
Controller-owned regression coverage for compatible accepted-task preservation during refreeze adoption.
Controller-owned public workflow export for finalization.
Controller-owned next-action projection for FINAL_AUDIT finalization.

requirements.description: The package shall provide a stable compact JSON CLI for specification, plan, task compilation, workflow, audit, adapter, conformance, neutrality, and schema operations, with full state available only by explicit request, no host-specific shell wrappers in core, and a thin dispatcher over modular controller services rather than production lifecycle semantics inside one monolithic script.

The workflow controller shall be implemented as a modular Python package with a thin CLI facade. Production lifecycle semantics shall not live in one large workflowctl.py-style script. The implementation shall keep one-way dependencies from CLI to API to use-case modules to contracts/ports, keep adapters outside core imports, and pass small-context size gates for source files, functions, dispatchers and launchers. SOLID boundaries are required for state transition, artifact store, operation publication, host adapter, usage attestation, authorization gate and validator execution; DRY is required for canonical JSON, digest, WAL, publication, receipt, path and capability logic; YAGNI forbids hosted control planes, mandatory MCP/app servers, provider-specific routing in core, copied project samples and live adapter promotion as a release prerequisite.

task.launchGate: LG-07 WS-03 through WS-06 accepted and the controller pre-launch gate passed
